title: DeletionCertificate (v1)
description: Signed retention deletion certificate. Single discriminated union by `kind`. Adding a new shape ships as an additive arm here plus a new dispatch arm in `verifyDeletionCertificate` — never a rename of an existing one.

kind.description: Discriminator. Interop law per protocol-model.md §'Naming: interop law vs reference default' — verifiers dispatch on this exact string; alternative implementations cannot rename.

target_id.description: The deleted record's identifier in the originating store (a NodeId for memory).

sensitivity.description: Sensitivity level of the deleted record at deletion time. Same closed union as `SensitivityLevel` in @motebit/protocol. Drives the retention-floor check the runtime applied before issuing the cert.

reason.description: Action class — constrains the permitted signer set per the reason × signer × mode table (decision 5). Verifiers admit a cert iff the present signature(s) match the row for this reason and the operator's declared deployment mode.

deleted_at.description: Unix milliseconds when the runtime erased the record.

motebit_id.description: The motebit whose identity key produced this signature.

suite.description: Cryptosuite identifier. Always `motebit-jcs-ed25519-b64-v1` for deletion certificates: JCS canonicalization (RFC 8785), Ed25519 primitive, base64url signature encoding, hex public-key encoding. Verifiers reject missing or unknown values fail-closed.

signature.description: Base64url-encoded Ed25519 signature over `canonicalJson(cert_body)` where `cert_body` is the cert with every `*_signature` field removed. All present signers sign identical bytes — mirrors identity-v1 §3.8.1 dual-signature succession.

operator_id.description: Stable identifier for the operator key in the operator's transparency manifest.

suite.description: Cryptosuite identifier. Always `motebit-jcs-ed25519-b64-v1` for deletion certificates: JCS canonicalization (RFC 8785), Ed25519 primitive, base64url signature encoding, hex public-key encoding. Verifiers reject missing or unknown values fail-closed.

signature.description: Base64url-encoded Ed25519 signature, same canonical bytes as `subject_signature.signature`.

motebit_id.description: The delegate's motebit identity (a third party authorized via delegation-v1.md §5.5).

delegation_receipt_id.description: Reference to the delegation receipt that authorized this scope. Verifiers MAY cross-check the receipt to confirm retention-scope authorization is in force.

suite.description: Cryptosuite identifier. Always `motebit-jcs-ed25519-b64-v1` for deletion certificates: JCS canonicalization (RFC 8785), Ed25519 primitive, base64url signature encoding, hex public-key encoding. Verifiers reject missing or unknown values fail-closed.

signature.description: Base64url-encoded Ed25519 signature, same canonical bytes.

guardian_public_key.description: Hex-encoded guardian Ed25519 public key. Verifiers MUST cross-check this against the subject motebit's `motebit.md` §3.3 `guardian.public_key` field — drift means the guardian binding is forged.

suite.description: Cryptosuite identifier. Always `motebit-jcs-ed25519-b64-v1` for deletion certificates: JCS canonicalization (RFC 8785), Ed25519 primitive, base64url signature encoding, hex public-key encoding. Verifiers reject missing or unknown values fail-closed.

signature.description: Base64url-encoded Ed25519 signature by the guardian over the same canonical bytes.

anyOf.description: Mutable pruning cert — interior-structure deletion (memory). Attests bytes-unrecoverable, not tombstone (decision 7). Verifiers check at-least-one-signature-required AND every present signature against canonical bytes.
Per-motebit horizon — signed by the motebit's identity key.
Operator-wide horizon — signed by the operator key.

store_id.description: Stable identifier for the audit log within the operator's deployment.

horizon_ts.description: Unix milliseconds. Entries with `timestamp < horizon_ts` are unrecoverable from this point on. Witnesses attest they accept the horizon at this value.

motebit_id.description: Witnessing motebit's identity. The pubkey resolves through the same `resolveMotebitPublicKey` path as subject signatures.

signature.description: Base64url-encoded Ed25519 signature over `canonicalJson(cert minus signature)` — the SAME bytes the issuer signed. Witnesses sign the body INCLUDING themselves, so the array layout at issuer-signing time is fixed.

siblings.description: Hex-encoded sibling hashes ordered leaf-to-root. Same shape as credential-anchor-v1 §6.

leaf_index.description: 0-based index of the witness's pubkey in the sorted peer set.

layer_sizes.description: Per-layer node counts, used to detect odd-leaf promotion when reconstructing the root.

inclusion_proof.description: Optional Merkle inclusion proof for the witness's pubkey against `federation_graph_anchor.merkle_root`. Phase 4 quorum mechanisms either require this or accept signature-only witnesses; reserved here so phase 4 lands without a wire break.

witnessed_by.description: Federation peers co-witnessing the horizon advance. Empty array is permitted only when `witness_required` was false at issuance time (decision 9 — derived from federation graph state).

algo.description: Closed registry of Merkle algorithm identifiers. `merkle-sha256-v1` body: SHA-256 leaves, binary tree with odd-leaf promotion (no duplication) per credential-anchor-v1 §3-5 + relay-federation-v1 §7.6, composed with this doctrine's peer-set canonicalization (operator's federation-peer Ed25519 public keys, hex-encoded lowercase, sorted ascending, at horizon_ts).

merkle_root.description: Hex-encoded SHA-256 root over the canonical peer-set leaves.

leaf_count.description: Number of peer pubkeys in the anchored set.

issued_at.description: Unix milliseconds when the issuer signed.

suite.description: Cryptosuite identifier. Always `motebit-jcs-ed25519-b64-v1` for deletion certificates: JCS canonicalization (RFC 8785), Ed25519 primitive, base64url signature encoding, hex public-key encoding. Verifiers reject missing or unknown values fail-closed.

signature.description: Base64url-encoded Ed25519 signature over `canonicalJson(cert minus signature)`. Same bytes every witness signed.

anyOf.description: Append-only horizon cert — whole-prefix truncation of an audit ledger. Subject discriminator names the issuer (per-motebit or operator-wide); both can coexist with `max` precedence (decision 8).

target_id.description: The flushed record's identifier in the originating store (a turn id, audit row id, etc.).

sensitivity.description: Sensitivity level of the deleted record at deletion time. Same closed union as `SensitivityLevel` in @motebit/protocol. Drives the retention-floor check the runtime applied before issuing the cert.

reason.description: Action class — constrains the permitted signer set per the reason × signer × mode table (decision 5). Verifiers admit a cert iff the present signature(s) match the row for this reason and the operator's declared deployment mode.

flushed_to.description: Disposition: `memory_node` if the flush produced a memory node (the node's `mutable_pruning` policy applies thereafter), `expire` if no derived structure was retained.

memory_node_id.description: Present iff `flushed_to: memory_node`. References the consolidated node so audit trails reconstruct the derivation.

motebit_id.description: The motebit whose identity key produced this signature.

suite.description: Cryptosuite identifier. Always `motebit-jcs-ed25519-b64-v1` for deletion certificates: JCS canonicalization (RFC 8785), Ed25519 primitive, base64url signature encoding, hex public-key encoding. Verifiers reject missing or unknown values fail-closed.

signature.description: Base64url-encoded Ed25519 signature over `canonicalJson(cert_body)` where `cert_body` is the cert with every `*_signature` field removed. All present signers sign identical bytes — mirrors identity-v1 §3.8.1 dual-signature succession.

operator_id.description: Stable identifier for the operator key in the operator's transparency manifest.

suite.description: Cryptosuite identifier. Always `motebit-jcs-ed25519-b64-v1` for deletion certificates: JCS canonicalization (RFC 8785), Ed25519 primitive, base64url signature encoding, hex public-key encoding. Verifiers reject missing or unknown values fail-closed.

signature.description: Base64url-encoded Ed25519 signature, same canonical bytes as `subject_signature.signature`.

motebit_id.description: The delegate's motebit identity (a third party authorized via delegation-v1.md §5.5).

delegation_receipt_id.description: Reference to the delegation receipt that authorized this scope. Verifiers MAY cross-check the receipt to confirm retention-scope authorization is in force.

suite.description: Cryptosuite identifier. Always `motebit-jcs-ed25519-b64-v1` for deletion certificates: JCS canonicalization (RFC 8785), Ed25519 primitive, base64url signature encoding, hex public-key encoding. Verifiers reject missing or unknown values fail-closed.

signature.description: Base64url-encoded Ed25519 signature, same canonical bytes.

guardian_public_key.description: Hex-encoded guardian Ed25519 public key. Verifiers MUST cross-check this against the subject motebit's `motebit.md` §3.3 `guardian.public_key` field — drift means the guardian binding is forged.

suite.description: Cryptosuite identifier. Always `motebit-jcs-ed25519-b64-v1` for deletion certificates: JCS canonicalization (RFC 8785), Ed25519 primitive, base64url signature encoding, hex public-key encoding. Verifiers reject missing or unknown values fail-closed.

signature.description: Base64url-encoded Ed25519 signature by the guardian over the same canonical bytes.

anyOf.description: Consolidation flush cert — surface-flow record disposition (conversation turn, tool-audit row). Multi-signature shape mirrors mutable_pruning; flushed_to discriminator names the disposition.
